title: narcissus cypri
intro: Narcissus cypri, also known as the Cypriot Daffodil, is a species of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its trumpet-shaped flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant's bulbs have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Narcissus cypri include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural antiseptic to treat wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Narcissus cypri often involve drying and grinding the bulbs into a powder, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or added to creams and ointments for topical application.
